title: FUGUEの実装を確認し、Kernelとの差分を埋めて。ただしFUGUE/kernel両方の既存のシステムを損壊しないように慎重に実装をマルチエージェントで進めて。
summary_text: 1. **High — Uninitialized policy variables become hard failure on the implement path** - `route-task-handoff.sh` only loads `compat_label`, `orchestrator_label`, `assist_orchestrator_label` if `orchestrator-policy.sh` runs successfully; otherwise it only sets `main_fallback_reason` and skips those exports. - The same script later unconditionally consumes those variables in implement flow label/comment logic. That triggers `set -u` abort for implement handoffs when the policy script is missing/inaccessible. - Files: [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L178), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L333), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L342), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L434), [scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh#L209) 2. **High — Handoff summary can be published before execution path is guaranteed** - `route-task-handoff.sh` writes the issue handoff comment (“consensus starts now…”) before adapter/workflow dispatch executes. - If adapter script checks/manifest checks fail after that point, the issue gets a false “already dispatched” state. - Files: [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L482), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L500), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L545) 3. **Medium — Explicit `handoff_target=kernel` has asymmetric failure behavior** - For `fugue-bridge`, missing/invalid bridge adapter script is treated as hard stop. - For `kernel`, missing `kernel-handoff.sh` falls through to direct `gh workflow run` instead of hard-stop, so target semantics are silently changed from adapter-mediated flow to direct caller dispatch. - Files: [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L545), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L577), [scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/route-task-handoff.sh#L604), [scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/kernel-handoff.sh#L130), [scripts/harness/fugue-bridge-handoff.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/harness/fugue-bridge-handoff.sh#L130) 4. **Medium — Explicit `fugue-bridge` handoff is not “independent”; caller rewrites orchestration policy anyway** - In `fugue-tutti-caller.yml`, when `handoff_target == fugue-bridge`, provider/mode knobs are forcibly overridden to legacy defaults before `fugue-tutti-router` invocation, rather than preserving caller-resolved policy. - This is an explicit coupling path that can change runtime behavior even when handoff target was set manually. - Files: [ .github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L569), [ .github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L570), [ .github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L573), [ .github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.github/workflows/fugue-tutti-caller.yml#L575) 5. **Low — Tests do not cover critical failure branches for handoff...
